title: Dryopteris emigrans
taxonomy: Dryopteris emigrans is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is classified within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida and the order Polypodiales. Within this order, Dryopteris emigrans is a member of the family Dryopteridaceae and the genus Dryopteris. Its classification is further divided into the species level, making it a distinct entity within the plant kingdom.

The plant contains compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Dryopteris emigrans.

intro: Dryopteris emigrans, also known as the autumn fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris emigrans include flavonoids, saponins, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the autumn fern is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its therapeutic benefits.
